news.title: Focus of Ahmedabad development plan shifts to 2041
news.para: Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority is drafting a 20-year Development Plan (DP 2041), a departure from the traditional 10-year cycle, driven by the prospect of hosting the 2036 Olympics. This extended plan aims to transform Ahmedabad into a global metropolitan city, with special provisions for sports facilities and infrastructure development in surrounding villages.

news.title: SC issues notice, orders status quo on Ramodaya housing project permissions in Mormugao
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued a notice to Goa's town and country planning department and the Mormugao planning authority regarding permissions granted for a housing society project. The court has also ordered a status quo on the property's possession and construction, following allegations of illegal hill cutting and violations of development regulations.

news.title: Credai moves Madras HC against construction ban near Pallikaranai
news.para: Credai has approached the Madras High Court challenging a National Green Tribunal order that prohibits construction within 1km of Pallikaranai marshland. The association argues the 'zone of influence' was arbitrarily fixed without scientific study or stakeholder consultation. The NGT order has led to a planning freeze across a vast, already developed area.

news.title: Pune civic body mandates sensors to monitor STPs in private housing societies
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC) has mandated nearly 750 housing societies to install sewage treatment plant (STP) sensors for efficient monitoring. This initiative aims to curb untreated wastewater release and reduce water pollution, with performance data to be monitored centrally. The move supports PMC's broader efforts to enhance sewage treatment capacity across the city.

news.title: Delhi: NDMC tells Palika Dham residents to vacate premises by March 7
news.para: New Delhi Municipal Council has issued an immediate evacuation notice for Palika Dham residents, declaring the building unsafe. A March 7 deadline is set for vacating the premises, with alternative accommodation offered through bidding. This directive follows a similar order in July, which residents had resisted.

news.title: Consumer commission orders Aditya Builders and Developers to execute deed of sale
news.para: A consumer commission has ordered Aditya Builders and Developers to execute a sale deed for a flat bought by Santosh and Swati Joshi in 2004. The firm must comply within two months. Failure will allow the couple to get the sale executed through the commission. The firm also faces a Rs 50,000 compensation order for delays and unresolved sewage issues.

news.title: Noida: Paramount Developers clears ₹106 crore dues, unlocks registry for 135 flats
news.para: Paramount Developers has paid over Rs 106 crore to Noida and Greater Noida authorities, clearing dues for two housing projects. This payment enables the registry of 135 flats, with 35 in Paramount Emotions and 100 in Paramount Floraville. The developer deposited the amount 'under protest' to facilitate buyer registrations.

news.title: Hyderabad: Case against builder for cheating buyers for over ₹5 crore in Tripura Nirvana project
news.para: A real estate company and its promoters are facing cheating charges for allegedly defrauding multiple home-buyers of over Rs 5.6 crore in a pre-launch project at Tellapur. One complainant paid Rs 77 lakh for a flat but received neither the property nor a refund, with allegations of the company attempting to transfer project land and evade buyers.

news.title: Himachal Pradesh government mandates installation of EV charging stations in commercial, public buildings
news.para: Himachal Pradesh is mandating the installation of electric vehicle charging stations in all new buildings and real estate developments. The government is also promoting energy efficiency measures, enabling developers to acquire additional building space under revised regulations.

news.title: Prime Offices Fund acquires 51% stake in 1.1 million sq ft office asset in Pune
news.para: PRIME Offices Fund has acquired a significant stake in a large office development in Pune. This move expands PRIME's office portfolio to nearly 3.8 million square feet. The fund manager, NCW, is a joint venture between Nuvama Asset Management and Cushman & Wakefield. This follows a recent major acquisition in Chennai.

news.title: The evolution of home loan insurance in India
news.para: In India, home loan insurance is undergoing a transformation. It has become an essential financial ally for homeowners. With more affordable options, borrowers can now shield their families from the burden of debt. The ability to personalize coverage allows individuals to tailor protection to their unique circumstances.

news.title: Ahmedabad development body can now allow development in agri zone at Gokuldham
news.para: After a dispute suspended permissions in 2014, the Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority chairman can now issue development and revised development permissions for agricultural land within the Gokuldham township. This authority covers over 10 lakh square metres in Sanand taluka, following a decade-long approval process for the township's layout and subsequent land integrations.

news.title: Smart city projects near completion in 10 Uttar Pradesh cities
news.para: Uttar Pradesh has achieved a significant milestone in its Smart City mission, with 99% of its 682 sanctioned projects now commissioned. Despite initial delays due to irregularities, all allocated funds have been utilized, and the remaining projects are expected to be operational by March 14. This success paves the way for potential central government funding for further city upgrades.

news.title: SC issues notice to Goa government, developer for construction of villas at Siolim
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued notices to the Goa government and a project developer. This follows a challenge to a High Court order that dismissed a plea against building eight luxury villas at Oxel in Siolim. The petitioner argues the project threatens the 363-year-old Oxel spring, a vital water source for residents facing a deficit. The court's decision is awaited.

news.title: Ghaziabad development body to take help of brokers, marketing cell to sell 1,700 flats
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority plans to sell over 1,700 unsold houses. Professional brokers will be hired to find buyers. A marketing cell will also be established. This strategy aims to generate over Rs 500 crore. The initiative will not apply to EWS flats. This follows a similar state government scheme.

news.title: HC asks Greater Noida authority to restore plot it cancelled nine years ago
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has reinstated a Rs 100 crore farmhouse plot to SDS Infratech, overturning a nine-year-old cancellation by the Greater Noida Authority. The court directed the authority to decide on the developer's zero-period relief claim within four months, quashing the 2017 cancellation order.

news.title: Cafes, restaurants on Noida abadi land bypassing fire NOCs, building bylaws, parking norms
news.para: Popular Noida nightlife hubs, built on abadi lands, are operating without fire safety clearances, posing a significant risk. Despite warnings from residents about narrow access and overloaded connections, authorities face limitations due to relaxed norms for establishments under 500 sq m, creating a dangerous regulatory gap.

news.title: Delhi mayor urges residents to clear dues under property tax amnesty scheme before March 31
news.para: Delhi Mayor Raja Iqbal Singh is encouraging property owners to use the SUNIYO 2025-26 property tax amnesty scheme. This scheme offers a waiver on past interest and penalties for dues before the financial year 2020-21. The deadline for this opportunity is March 31, 2026. Over 1.8 lakh taxpayers have already benefited.
